2nd Transnational Project Meeting

TranSustain HE Convenes in Skopje to Advance Its Sustainable University Evaluation Model

📅 12–13 June 2026📍 International Balkan University, Skopje🇪🇺 Erasmus+ KA220-HED

The TranSustain HE consortium gathered in Skopje, North Macedonia, for its 2nd Transnational Project Meeting - bringing together all seven partner institutions from five countries to review progress, present the project's draft evaluation model, and chart the roadmap towards Work Packages 3 and 4.

Hosted by International Balkan University (IBU) on 12–13 June 2026, the meeting marked a key milestone in the project's journey. Co-funded by the European Union under the Erasmus+ KA220-HED Programme (Project No. 2025-1-TR01-KA220-HED-000362010) and coordinated by Necmettin Erbakan University (Türkiye), TranSustain HE - Transforming Sustainability in Higher Education with a Data-Driven Digital Assessment System - aims to develop an integrated, data-driven model for assessing sustainability performance in higher education institutions.

A Warm Welcome at IBU

The meeting opened with a warm welcome from the host institution. IBU Rector Prof. Dr. Lütfi Sunar delivered the opening address, underlining the importance of sustainability in higher education and the value of strong, multi-partner collaboration, and wishing the consortium success in the implementation of the project. Vice-Rector Assoc. Prof. Dr. Katerina Mitevska Petrusheva outlined the two-day programme, and Project Coordinator Assoc. Prof. Dr. Özdal Koyuncuoğlu (Necmettin Erbakan University) thanked IBU for its generous hospitality before opening the working sessions. The agenda was presented by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Saadet Korucu Kış and approved by the consortium.

Progress So Far

In the first session, Prof. Dr. Fırat Yaldız (Necmettin Erbakan University) presented a comprehensive review of the work completed since the Kick-Off Meeting in Konya (October 2025). Highlights included the consortium's expansion with the admission of Ankara University and Selçuk University, together with the revision of the Grant Agreement; five productive monthly online meetings; the collection of country-based analyses of universities and their sustainability offices; the successful WP5 expert workshop hosted by Ankara University on 8 May 2026; systematic dissemination through the project website, social media and partner institutions; and the extension of Work Package 2 to 30 June 2026.

Scenes from the opening session.

The Draft Evaluation Model Takes Centre Stage

At the heart of the meeting was the second session, led by the Project Coordinator, which unveiled the project's central output: the draft Sustainable University Evaluation Model. Developed within Work Packages 2 and 3 and accompanied by a methodological handbook, the model offers a comprehensive new framework for measuring how universities perform on sustainability.

Presented from its guiding philosophy to its practical application, the model offers a holistic framework that brings together the environmental, social and economic dimensions of sustainability and aligns them with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. Its distinctive approach combines objective institutional data with the perceptions of a university's own stakeholders, offering a more complete and balanced picture of sustainability performance.

The presentation prompted a rich and constructive discussion. Drawing on their wide-ranging academic expertise, the partners offered valuable perspectives on developing the model - from the design of its measurement scales to the selection and prioritisation of indicators, including a proposal to run a structured expert (Delphi) study to strengthen the process. The consortium emphasised that the model is a draft starting point whose refinement and validation will continue through expert consultation in the months ahead, with the aim of delivering a robust, transparent and widely applicable tool for higher education.

Partners in focused discussion throughout the two-day working sessions at IBU.

Looking Ahead

  • Finalising the project's foundational research and the country-level analyses of universities in the weeks ahead.
  • Refining and validating the draft evaluation model through continued expert consultation, including a planned Delphi study.
  • Advancing the project's data-driven digital assessment system - the heart of TranSustain HE.
  • Reconvening for the 3rd Transnational Project Meeting in Ankara in November 2026.
Project materials and the welcoming setting at the host university, International Balkan University.

A Tour of the IBU Campus

Beyond the working sessions, the host university opened its doors for a guided tour of the IBU campus. Partners visited the university's modern teaching and research facilities - including its media and broadcasting studio, health-sciences simulation labs and anatomy laboratories - gaining first-hand insight into IBU's learning environment and its commitment to a future-ready, sustainable campus.

Exploring International Balkan University's modern teaching and research facilities during the campus tour.

Networking and a Memorable Social Programme

Beyond the working sessions, the programme offered rich opportunities for networking and team-building. Participants enjoyed a guided tour of the IBU campus, a group photo in front of the Rectorate, and a walk through Skopje's historic city centre. On the second day, the consortium travelled to the breathtaking Matka Canyon for a nature hike, a boat tour to the Vrelo Cave and a group lunch - strengthening the friendship and collaboration that drive the project forward.

Team-building at the Matka Canyon strengthened the partnership.

Certificate Presentation Ceremony

← swipe to browse all the certificate photos →
Certificates of participation were presented to the partners during the meeting.

With a clear roadmap and renewed energy, the TranSustain HE consortium continues its work towards a more sustainable, data-driven future for higher education. The journey continues in Ankara in November 2026.
